BUYER LETTERHEAD
DATE
CONFIDENTIAL
NAME, ADDRESS OF SELLER
RE: Acquisition of XXXX
Dear Mr. ____________:
The purpose of this letter of intent is to set forth certain general terms of a proposed transaction between AAA.,
a [CCC] corporation (“AAA”), and BBB, an _________ corporation (the “Company” or “BBB” or “Seller”).
The transaction is subject to satisfactory completion by AAA of a diligence review of the Company, and the
execution and delivery by AAA, and the Company of a mutually satisfactory purchase agreement (the
“Purchase Agreement”).
1. 	The principal terms of the proposed transaction shall, subject to the terms of this Letter of Intent be
substantially as follows:
a. 	Seller shall sell and AAA shall purchase certain assets (and the good will relating thereto)
pertaining to and the essential components of a __________________________ including all applicable
code, data, customer lists, customer agreements, trademarks, copyrights and proprietary information all
free and clear of any security interests, mortgages, or other encumbrances (collectively the “Assets”).
b. 	AAA shall pay Seller consideration for the purchase as follows:
i.AAA shall pay Seller $_________ USD at Closing;
ii.AAA shall execute a $___________ Note at Closing, with ___% interest with a term of ____ (__) months;
payments commencing thirty (30) days after Closing and prepayment allowed without penalty;
iii.AAA shall pay the sum of $________ as follows:
2. 	AAA’s Financing of the Transaction. AAA will finance the purchase internally. There will be no
financing contingency.
Sample LOI Asset Purchase
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 5 --

3. 	Due Diligence Review. Promptly following the execution of this letter of intent, the Company will
allow AAA to complete AAA’s examination of the Company’s financial, accounting, business record, contracts,
and other legal documents to complete due diligence. Any information obtained by AAA as a result thereof will
be maintained by AAA in confidence subject to the terms of the Confidentiality Agreement executed by the
Company and AAA. The parties will cooperate to complete due diligence expeditiously.
4. 	Definitive Purchase Agreement. All of the terms and conditions of the proposed transaction would be
stated in the Purchase Agreement, to be negotiated, agreed and executed by the Company and AAA. Neither
party intends to be bound by any oral or written statements or correspondence concerning the Purchase
Agreement arising during the course of negotiations, notwithstanding that the same may be expressed in terms
signifying a partial, preliminary or interim agreement between the parties.
5. 	Conduct of Business in the Ordinary Course. In addition to the conditions discussed in this letter of
intent and others to be contained in the Purchase Agreement, consummation of the transaction would be subject
to the Company having conducted its business in the ordinary course in accordance with historical practices
between the date of this letter of intent and the date of Closing and there having been no material adverse change
in the Company’s business, assets, liabilities, financial condition or prospects. The Company will use reasonable
efforts to maintain its relations with all employees, suppliers and customers and conduct its business in a proper,
prudent and lawful manner in keeping with its current practices so as to protect its assets, business, goodwill
and prospects. The Company will continue to maintain insurance coverage on its assets and operations at
existing levels. Except for assignments, transfers and other dispositions in the ordinary course of business,
during the Exclusivity Period (defined below), the Company will not dispose of, assign, transfer or encumber
in any manner any of its assets or resources of the business without the prior written consent of AAA.
6. 	Continuation of Employment. AAA intends to cause the Company to continue to employ, or
alternatively offer employment to, certain of the Company’s employees, and would expect the Company to use
reasonable efforts to assist AAA in retaining, or employing, these individuals, and will assist AAA in its or the
Company’s efforts to enter into employment agreements with certain key employees of the Company, which
will include employment provisions satisfactory to AAA and such employees and a continuation of the existing
non-compete restrictions of such key employees.
7. 	Acceptance and Closing. It is currently anticipated that the Closing would occur on or before
_______________________, following the Company’s timely acceptance of this letter of intent, the completion
of AAA’s due diligence review and the preparation and negotiation of the Purchase Agreement and related
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 5 --

documents. In the interests of ensuring that there is sufficient time to conclude all matters necessary for a Closing
on or before [INSERT CLOSING DATE], this proposal shall remain open until 5:00 p.m. EST on
_______________. If this letter of intent is not signed by all parties and received by AAA before such time, this
offer shall lapse and become null and void and no party shall have any obligation to any other hereunder.
8. 	Expenses. AAA and the Company shall each pay its own expenses, including but not limited to legal,
accounting and other professional advisory fees, in connection with the transactions contemplated by this letter
of intent.
9. 	Public Announcements. Neither the Company nor AAA will make any announcement of the proposed
transaction contemplated by this letter of intent prior to the execution of the Purchase Agreement without the
prior written approval of the other, which approval will not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. AAA and the
Company will jointly announce the transaction after the Purchase Agreement has been executed. The foregoing
shall not restrict in any respect the Company’s or AAA’s ability to communicate information concerning this
letter of intent and the transactions contemplated hereby to their respective affiliates and its and their respective
officers, directors, employees and professional advisers, and, to the extent relevant, to third parties whose
consent is required in connection with the transaction contemplated by this letter of intent.
10. 	Exclusive Negotiating Rights. In order to induce AAA to commit the resources, forego other potential
opportunities, and incur the legal, accounting, and incidental expenses necessary properly to evaluate the
possibility of acquiring the Equity Interests and Assets as described above, the Company agrees that from the
date of this letter of intent through ____________, 2020, none of the Company, their affiliates or any of their
respective officers, directors, employees and agents shall initiate, solicit, encourage, directly or indirectly, or
accept any offer or proposal, regarding the possible acquisition by any person or entity other than AAA,
including, without limitation, by way of a purchase of shares, purchase of assets or merger, of all or any
substantial part of the Company’s equity securities or assets, and shall not (other than in the ordinary course of
business as heretofore conducted) AAA any confidential information regarding the Company’s assets or
business to any person other than AAA and AAA’s representatives.
11. 	Next Steps. After the parties execute this letter of intent, the next steps would be for AAA to
immediately initiate its due diligence review while concurrently commencing work on the preparation of a
Purchase Agreement, the initial draft of which will be prepared by AAA’s counsel. The general terms and
conditions of the transaction as defined in this document would form the basis for such Purchase Agreement.
Issues (if any) uncovered during the due diligence review would be negotiated and addressed within the
Purchase Agreement. The Purchase Agreement will be governed by CCC law.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

12. 	Binding Effect. Except for paragraphs 5, 6, and 11 through 14 (the “Binding Provisions”), this letter of
intent does not constitute a binding agreement among the parties but merely expresses their intent with respect
to the transactions contemplated hereby. A binding agreement regarding the transactions contemplated hereby
will be created only upon the execution and delivery of the Purchase Agreement by the parties thereto.
Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligations of the parties pursuant to the Binding Provisions are intended to
be binding and enforceable obligations. The Binding Provisions shall survive any termination of this letter of
intent and shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and
permitted assigns.
13. 	Miscellaneous. This letter shall be governed by the substantive laws of the State of CCC without regard
to conflict of law principles. This letter constitutes the entire understanding and agreement between the parties
hereto and their affiliates with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous
agreements, representations, warranties and understandings of such parties (whether oral or written). No
promise, inducement, representation or agreement, other than as expressly set forth herein, has been made to or
by the parties hereto. This letter may be amended only by written agreement, signed by the parties to be bound
by the amendment. This letter shall be construed according to its fair meaning and not strictly for or against
either party.
14. 	Contact Information.
Legal Contact Information.
AAA’s attorney for this transaction is:
BBB’s attorney is:
If the foregoing terms and conditions are acceptable to you, please so indicate by initialing each page and
signing the enclosed copy of this letter and returning it to the attention of the undersigned.
